scores.correctness_notes: Covers the change/task model, state persistence, ready boolean, and Python client usage correctly, but omits significant portions of the gold standard: CLI commands (pebble changes/tasks), REST API endpoints (/v1/changes/{id}, /v1/changes/{id}/wait, filtering with select=), change-update Notices via long-poll, and the full change structure fields (id, kind, summary, spawn-time, etc.).
scores.hallucination_notes: container.pebble.get_change() and container.pebble.wait_change() use a questionable access pattern — in ops the pebble client methods are typically accessed directly on the Container object or via ops.pebble.Client, not through a .pebble attribute. The change.err attribute may not exist under that name. Minor inaccuracies rather than clear fabrications.
